raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":1.0,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the passage stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the motivation, hypothesis, results, and conclusion form a persuasive argument chain.","R1.3":"The passage makes performance and ablation claims, so strengthening it requires ensuring the claims track the reported evidence.","R1.4":"Because the artifact asserts benchmark superiority and novelty of the consistency probe, contribution delta is a central responsibility.","R1.5":"The request can reasonably include improving the section-level narrative arc from problem to prior work to method to results.","R1.6":"A stronger version would necessarily improve sentence-level force, clarity, concision, and academic register.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unsupported empirical and bibliographic claims.","RX.2":"The request requires avoiding inflated claims such as strongest published method unless warranted by evidence.","RX.3":"The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not executing edits or evaluating the artifact in detail.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns detecting human versus synthetic respondents, downstream misuse and harms should remain in scope.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the artifact's stated claims, datasets, benchmarks, and citations."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the passage stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the motivation, hypothesis, results, and conclusion form a persuasive argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The passage makes performance and ablation claims, so strengthening it requires ensuring the claims track the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.4: Because the artifact asserts benchmark superiority and novelty of the consistency probe, contribution delta is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.5: The request can reasonably include improving the section-level narrative arc from problem to prior work to method to results.
rationale.R1.6: A stronger version would necessarily improve sentence-level force, clarity, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unsupported empirical and bibliographic claims.
rationale.RX.2: The request requires avoiding inflated claims such as strongest published method unless warranted by evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not executing edits or evaluating the artifact in detail.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns detecting human versus synthetic respondents, downstream misuse and harms should remain in scope.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the artifact's stated claims, datasets, benchmarks, and citations.
